title: Dr. Howard Chang addresses San Francisco Schiller Institute event
description:
Dr. Howard Chang - He is a leading civil engineer with significant international experience, including on the Three Gorges Dam. Within the general consulting areas of hydraulics, hydrology, sedimentation, and fluvial processes, he has been active in floodplain mapping, channel design, river channel erosion and sedimentation, hydrological simulation, and watershed analysis. He is a leading expert in water and river development projects, and remains a critical resource to developing such projects as the North American Water and Power Alliance, the Kra Canal, and other major water diversion projects globally.

The Schiller Institute's Strategic Seminar in San Franciso on June 8, drew 70 guests and experts to discuss the urgent topic "Will the U.S. Join the New Silk Road? Global Scientific Development or Nuclear War." The question is whether to join the One Belt, One Road world infrastructure plan put forward by China, or stick with the collapsing Western economies, whose bankruptcy is fueling the danger of a global nuclear war.

See the full proceedings here: lpac.co/sf-schiller-seminar

chapters.title: The Annual Precipitation of East Asia
